scheme_name: Saraswati Vidya Yojana: Free Distribution of Bicycles
page_title: Saraswati Vidya Yojana: Free Distribution of Bicycles

details: “Free Distribution of Bicycles'' under the “Saraswati Vidya Yojana”has been implemented by the Directorate of Education, U.T. Administration Of DNH & DD. Under this scheme, bicycles are provided free of cost to all the girl students, studying in class VIII(8) each year in the Govt. & Govt. aided schools/ Institutes of the U.T. Administration.

The UT Administration of Dadra & Nagar Haveli and Daman & Diu has launched “Saraswati Vidya Yojana” on 25th February, 2021, for the girl students of UT of Dadra & Nagar Haveli and Daman & Diu. The scheme aims to encourage girl students to continue education keeping in view the aim of:- “Beti Bachao Beti Padhao”.
Objective of the "Free Distribution of Bicycles" Sub-scheme:

The objective of the scheme is to retain the girl students at Secondary & Higher Secondary stage and thereby decreasing the Dropout of the girl students by providing Bicycles to the girl students of class VIII to commute to school.
benefits: Bicycles will be provided to all the girl students.
eligibility: The applicant should be a girl.
The applicant should be a student.
The applicant should be studying in class VIII(8) in Government and Government Aided School of the UT of Dadra & Nagar Haveli and Daman & Diu.

offline: The Head Master/ In-charge Head Master will provide the number of enrolment of Girls in Std. VIII in Government and Government Aided Schools to the District Education Office.
The girl student does not required to make any application individually.
Bicycles will be provided to schools at the time of commencement of the New Academic Year as per the requirement of the respective schools.
District Education Office (Silvassa), PWD Campus, Silvassa, Pin: 396230, Ph: 0260-2642098, Email: doe-dnh@nic.in

faqs.question: Who are the target beneficiaries of the scheme?
faqs.answer: The target beneficiaries of the scheme are girl students of class VIII.

faqs.question: Which schools are eligible under this scheme?
faqs.answer: The scheme applies to students from Government and Government Aided Schools in the Union Territories of Dadra and Nagar Haveli (D&NH) and Daman and Diu (DD).

faqs.question: Are private school students eligible for this scheme?
faqs.answer: No, only students from Government and Government Aided Schools are eligible for this scheme.

faqs.question: Is the scheme limited to any specific class?
faqs.answer: Yes, the scheme is specifically for girl students of class VIII.

faqs.question: Which regions are covered under this scheme?
faqs.answer: The scheme covers the Union Territories of Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu.

faqs.question: What are the consequences of making a false declaration in the scholarship application?
faqs.answer: Making a false declaration in the scholarship application can lead to penalties. The applicant may be required to repay the scholarship amount and could also face legal actions.

faqs.question: Who is eligible to receive the bicycle?
faqs.answer: Girl students of Class VIII in Government and Government Aided Schools of this Union Territory are eligible to receive the bicycle.

faqs.question: How is the number of bicycles required determined?
faqs.answer: The Head Master/ In-charge Head Master will provide the number of enrolled girls in Class VIII to the District Education Office.

faqs.question: When will the bicycles be provided?
faqs.answer: Bicycles will be provided to schools at the commencement of the New Academic Year based on the requirements of the respective schools.

faqs.question: Who is responsible for distributing the bicycles?
faqs.answer: The Head Master/In-charge Head Master of each school is responsible for the distribution of bicycles to the eligible girl students.

faqs.question: Does the girl student need to apply individually to receive the bicycle?
faqs.answer: No, the girl student does not need to make any individual application to receive the bicycle.

faqs.question: Can a girl student avail the bicycle benefit more than once?
faqs.answer: No, once a girl student has availed the bicycle benefit, she will not be eligible to receive it again in any subsequent year.

faqs.question: Who has the authority to make inquiries about the usage of the bicycles?
faqs.answer: The Headmasters/Principals of Middle Schools, High Schools, and Higher Secondary Schools have the authority to make necessary inquiries regarding the usage of the bicycles.

faqs.question: What actions can be taken if a bicycle provided under this scheme is found to be sold?
faqs.answer: The Directorate of Education and District Offices have the right to take legal action against the girl's parents if it is found that the bicycle has been sold.
